sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me examine the frame structure more carefully
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Look at the full bottom-right region including frame
print("Bottom-right grid with frame (rows 34-58, cols 32-58):")
for r in range(34, min(58, len(ascii_lines))):
    row_str = ascii_lines[r][32:58]
    if any(c not in 'B' for c in row_str):
        print(f"  Row {r}: '{row_str}'")

# Try clicking on the charcoal frame
# The frame appears to be at the edges of the grid
# Let me try clicking on the frame between cells

# Try clicking on the horizontal divider between row 0 and row 1

r1 = last_action_result
print(f"Frame click (42, 40): board_changed={r1.get('board_changed', False)}")

# Try clicking on the gray corner
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 34, 'col': 34}])
r2 = last_action_result
print(f"Corner click (34, 34): board_changed={r2.get('board_changed', False)}")

# Try making ALL cells red
print("\nTrying all-red pattern...")
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 38},  # (0,0)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 46},  # (0,1)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 54},  # (0,2)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 38},  # (1,0)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 54},  # (1,2)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 38},  # (2,0)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 46},  # (2,1)
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 54},  # (2,2)
])
r3 = last_action_result
print(f"All red: board_changed={r3.get('board_changed', False)}, done={r3.get('done', False)}, level_completed={r3.get('level_completed', False)}")

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nBottom-right grid (all red attempt):")
for label, r_start in [("Row 0", 36), ("Row 1", 44), ("Row 2", 52)]:
    row_str = ascii_lines[r_start][36:58]
    print(f"  {label}: '{row_str}'")

# If all-red didn't work, let me check the center cell patterns more carefully
if not r3.get('level_completed') and not r3.get('done'):
    print("\nAll-red failed. Let me examine center cells more carefully...")
    # Reset to all blue first
    action([
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 38},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 46},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 54},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 38},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 54},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 38},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 46},
        {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 54},
    ])
    print("Reset to all blue")
    
    # Now let me look at the center cell patterns using segmentation
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    nodes = seg['nodes']
    print("\nObjects in bottom-right grid area (color, pixels, boundary):")
    for n in nodes:
        # Check if object is in bottom-right area
        b = n['boundary']
        if b:
            min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
            max_r = max(p[0] for p in b)
            min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
            max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
            if min_r >= 34 and min_c >= 34:
                print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary=[{min_r},{min_c}]-[{max_r},{max_c}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
